text.title: sota-onboarding-1day-runbook Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of sota-onboarding-1day-runbook enforces.

rule.statement: The runbook fits an 8-hour wall-clock budget on a single calendar day. Stages: 1h announce-scan + spec read, 1h gateway adapter wiring, 1h smoke eval, 2h full benchmark vs incumbent, 1h cost-quality readout, 1h GO/NO-GO + decision record. Total never exceeds 8h.
rule.rationale: Without a wall-clock budget the onboarding stretches to 3 weeks and the team ships against the previous SOTA.

rule.statement: The new model is integrated through the existing model-gateway adapter pattern — never wired directly into a feature module. The adapter implements the same interface as the incumbent.
rule.rationale: Direct integrations create per-feature switching cost that compounds across the codebase.

rule.statement: The benchmark is run against the team's own stratified eval set, NOT against public leaderboards or vendor announcement numbers.
rule.rationale: Public scores over-index on capabilities that may not matter for the team's product.

rule.statement: The day ends with a written 1-page decision record: model id + version, eval-set scores vs incumbent, per-1k-token cost delta, p50/p95 latency delta, decision (GO / NO-GO / DEFER), named owner who will revisit. Committed to the repo.
rule.rationale: Decision records prevent re-running the same evaluation 4 months later.

rule.statement: A GO decision results in the model behind a feature flag routing 1-5% of traffic — never a direct cut-over. Promotion to 100% requires a separate later decision after >=48h of production traces.
rule.rationale: One-day evals miss long-tail failure modes.

rule.statement: Skip when the release is a minor point version or when the team has no eval set or is in an active incident.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
